definitions: A ghetto-transit camp established by the SS during World War II in the fortress town of Terez칤n, located in the Protectorate of Bohemia and Moravia (now the Czech Republic).
A propaganda camp used by the Nazis to deceive international observers about the true nature of the 'Final Solution'.
examples: Many artists and intellectuals were interned in Theresienstadt.
The Red Cross visited Theresienstadt, unaware of the deception.
Survivors of Theresienstadt later shared their testimonies.
